skill.name: volcano-plot-labeler
skill.description: Automatically identify and label the top significant genes in volcano plots using a repulsion algorithm that prevents text label overlap.
skill.summary: Generates publication-ready volcano plots with non-overlapping gene labels from differential expression data.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 77 static analysis findings are false positives caused by pattern matching on markdown documentation content. Backtick code examples in SKILL.md were flagged as external commands, the word 'algorithm' was misclassified as weak cryptography, and security checklist items mentioning '../' were flagged as path traversal. The actual Python script (scripts/main.py) is a legitimate data visualization tool with no network calls, no obfuscated code, no command injection, and no data exfiltration. It reads CSV/TSV files and generates volcano plot PNG images. No prompt injection attempts were found. Real risk is limited to standard file system operations for a data analysis skill.

low_findings.title: Markdown backtick code examples flagged as command execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: All 60 external_commands detections are inline code or code block examples in SKILL.md documentation. These are usage examples (e.g., pip install, python scripts/main.py) that instruct users how to run the tool. No actual command execution occurs from the markdown file itself.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm misclassification
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static analyzer flagged the word 'algorithm' in 'repulsion algorithm' and 'significance algorithm' as weak cryptographic algorithms. These refer to a force-directed label placement algorithm for volcano plots, not cryptography.

low_findings.title: Security checklist items flagged as path traversal
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Lines 223 and 226 in SKILL.md are security checklist items that describe protections against path traversal (e.g., 'No unauthorized file system access (../)'). These are documentation of security controls, not actual path traversal code.

low_findings.title: Source URL flagged as hardcoded network endpoint
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: SKILL.md line 7 contains a GitHub source URL in documentation. This is a citation/attribution link, not a network call. The actual Python script makes no HTTP requests.

low_findings.title: Documentation text flagged as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Lines flagged as 'system reconnaissance' contain parameter descriptions, test case labels, and documentation of edge cases. These are standard documentation patterns, not reconnaissance commands.

content.user_title: Label Top Genes in Volcano Plots Automatically
content.value_statement: Manually annotating volcano plots is slow and produces overlapping text labels. This skill automatically identifies the most significant genes and places clear, non-overlapping labels using a force-directed repulsion algorithm.
actual_capabilities: Reads CSV and TSV files containing differential expression results with log2 fold change and p-value columns
Automatically ranks and selects the top N most significant genes by combined statistical score
Generates publication-ready volcano plots with non-overlapping gene labels using a force-directed repulsion algorithm
Color-codes genes as upregulated, downregulated, or not significant based on configurable thresholds
Exports plots in PNG format with configurable DPI and figure dimensions
Provides both Python API and command-line interface for integration into analysis pipelines
limitations: Requires pre-computed differential expression data with log2 fold change and p-value columns
Label placement quality depends on data density and may need iteration tuning for crowded plots
Does not perform statistical analysis or gene set enrichment; input data must be pre-computed
Limited to labeling a single set of genes per plot; multi-group comparisons require separate runs

use_cases.title: Identify Key Biomarkers from RNA-seq Data
use_cases.description: A research scientist has DESeq2 output with 20,000 genes and needs to quickly identify and visualize the top differentially expressed genes for a grant proposal.

use_cases.title: Standardize Publication Figures Across Experiments
use_cases.description: A biostatistician needs to generate consistent volcano plots for 10 treatment comparisons with identical labeling style, threshold parameters, and output resolution.

use_cases.title: Create Figures for Thesis or Manuscript
use_cases.description: A graduate student needs publication-quality volcano plots with clean, non-overlapping gene labels for their thesis chapter on differential gene expression.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Volcano Plot with Default Settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a volcano plot from my differential expression data. The file is at data/deseq2_results.csv. Use default thresholds (p-value 0.05, log2FC 1.0) and label the top 10 genes with non-overlapping text.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Thresholds and Gene Selection
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a volcano plot from data/results.csv. I need stricter thresholds: p-value cutoff of 0.01 and log2FC cutoff of 1.5. Label the top 15 genes. Save the output as png/volcano.png at 600 DPI for journal submission.
prompt_templates.scenario: Experienced user with specific publication requirements

prompt_templates.title: Publication-Quality Figure with Custom Styling
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a volcano plot from treatment_vs_control.csv with the following specifications: p-value column is 'padj', log2FC column is 'log2FoldChange', gene column is 'gene_name'. Use a figure size of 12x10 inches, label fontsize 11, and add the title 'Treatment A vs Control: Day 14'. Save as PDF.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user preparing figures for publication

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Panel Comparison Across Conditions
prompt_templates.prompt: I have three CSV files comparing different time points: day7.csv, day14.csv, and day21.csv. For each file, generate a volcano plot with p-value threshold 0.05, log2FC threshold 1.0, top_n 10, and save each as a separate PNG. Use descriptive titles like 'Day 7: Treatment vs Control'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Power user processing multiple datasets in batch

output_examples.input: DESeq2 results CSV with columns: gene_name, log2FoldChange, padj. Contains 20,000 gene entries across two conditions (treatment vs control).
output: A volcano plot PNG file showing differentially expressed genes as scatter points. Red points indicate significantly upregulated genes (log2FC >= 1.0, padj < 0.05). Blue points indicate significantly downregulated genes (log2FC <= -1.0, padj < 0.05). Gray points represent non-significant genes.
The top 10 most significant genes are labeled with their gene names using non-overlapping text labels connected to their data points by thin leader lines.
Dashed threshold lines at x = -1.0, x = 1.0, and y = 1.3 (-log10(0.05)) mark the significance boundaries.

output_examples.input: Custom CSV with columns: gene_symbol, log2FC, pvalue. User requests top 15 labels, p-value threshold 0.01, log2FC threshold 2.0, figure size 14x12.
output: A volcano plot with stricter significance thresholds. Fewer points pass the higher cutoff, resulting in a cleaner visualization.
The top 15 gene labels are positioned with the repulsion algorithm, ensuring no text overlaps even with the larger label count.
Labels include white background boxes with rounded corners for readability against the scatter points.

best_practices: Inspect the input CSV columns before running to verify column names match the expected log2FC, p-value, and gene identifier columns.
For dense volcano plots with many significant genes, increase repulsion iterations to 200 or more to ensure labels find stable non-overlapping positions.
Review the labeled output for any remaining label overlap or truncation at plot edges before submitting for publication.
anti_patterns: Requesting more gene labels than the dataset contains or than can be reasonably displayed, causing excessive overlap or cluttered output.
Using default p-value and log2FC thresholds without considering the domain-specific significance criteria for the biological context.
Running the script on raw count data or normalized expression values instead of pre-computed differential expression results with proper fold change and p-value columns.

faq.question: What input data format does this skill require?
faq.answer: The skill requires a CSV or TSV file with columns for log2 fold change, p-value (adjusted or raw), and gene names. This data typically comes from differential expression tools like DESeq2, edgeR, or limma.

faq.question: How does the repulsion algorithm work?
faq.answer: The algorithm places labels at gene coordinates and iteratively applies forces: repulsion pushes overlapping labels apart, spring force pulls labels toward their gene, and boundary forces keep labels inside the plot area.

faq.question: Can I control which genes get labeled?
faq.answer: The skill labels the top N genes ranked by a combined significance score (abs(log2FC) * -log10(p-value)). You can control N with the top-n parameter but cannot manually specify individual genes.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: The script saves plots in PNG format by default. The Python API can be used to display figures interactively or save in other formats supported by matplotlib.

faq.question: Does this skill perform statistical tests?
faq.answer: No. The skill does not calculate p-values or fold changes. It requires pre-computed differential expression results from tools such as DESeq2, edgeR, or limma.

faq.question: What if the script fails to execute?
faq.answer: The script validates input file existence and required columns before processing. If it fails, check that all Python dependencies are installed (pandas, numpy, matplotlib) and that the input file has the expected column names.
